mnemonics: SOCKETS: Server Opens, Client Knows, Endpoints Talk Securely
keyPoints: Sockets provide low-level network communication endpoints.
ServerSocket listens for connections, Socket initiates them.
NIO (New I/O) offers non-blocking, scalable network operations via channels and selectors.
Security (SSL/TLS) is crucial for protecting data in transit over networks.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to close Sockets and ServerSockets, leading to resource leaks.
Using blocking I/O for a large number of clients, causing scalability issues.
Mixing up the roles of ServerSocket (for accepting) and Socket (for communicating).
